fields.summary: SonarQube setup needed for Python Flask microservice - coverage not showing
fields.description: We have a Python Flask service and want to get SonarQube running for it. Our main problem is that the scanner runs but shows 0.0% coverage even though our pytest tests do run and pass. We are generating a coverage.xml file using pytest-cov but SonarQube does not seem to pick it up. Nobody on the team has done Python SonarQube setup before and we could not find a clear guide internally.

content.text: Our sonar-project.properties currently has sonar.sources=. and sonar.language=py but nothing about coverage paths. The coverage.xml is being generated in the project root directory.

You are missing the sonar.python.coverage.reportPaths=coverage.xml property. This is the same issue I helped another team with last month for their Django service. Add that property to your sonar-project.properties and the coverage will appear. This question comes up every time a Python team onboards. We really need a Python SonarQube setup guide on the wiki.
That fixed it. Coverage showing at 74% now. I agree a wiki page would prevent this being raised every time. Closing the ticket.
